title: Melandrium preslii
taxonomy: Melandrium preslii is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. This family includes several species of flowering plants commonly known as pinks or carnations. Melandrium preslii is also known to be a synonym for Silene preslii, which is a species of flowering plant in the genus Silene. The genus Silene is a member of the order Caryophyllales, which includes various families of flowering plants. Melandrium preslii is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia.

intro: Melandrium preslii, also known as Alpine Pink Campion, is a flowering plant species within the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Melandrium preslii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Melandrium preslii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
